hook.text: In product-led growth companies, the product itself is the sales team. PMs own the entire customer journey.

buildup.text: PLG companies like Slack, Dropbox, and Figma let users try the product free — then convert through value.

discovery.text: PLG PMs think like growth marketers: funnels, activation metrics, onboarding friction, and viral loops.

twist.text: This blurs the line between product and marketing. PLG PMs need skills that traditional PMs never learned.

climax.text: As more companies adopt PLG, the PM role is expanding — from feature builder to full-funnel owner.

punchline.text: When the product sells itself, the PM owns everything.

quiz.question: What makes PLG product management different?
options: PMs own the entire customer journey, including acquisition and conversion
